access.anon: Can browse frequent links and follow public visit redirects but cannot pin them.
access.user: Can pin and unpin links; authenticated POST visits record click counts before redirecting.
access.admin: Has no dedicated link management interface separate from regular users.
access.agent: Can list/search dashboard links and manage the authenticated user's pin state through MCP; visit redirects remain browser/REST navigation.

rules.navigation-not-feed: Frequent links are a navigation capability, not a content feed.
rules.search-fuzzy: Search supports Chinese, pinyin, and whitespace-tolerant matching.
rules.links-vs-buttons: Links are for navigation; buttons are for actions.
rules.pin-limit: Currently a maximum of 4 links can be pinned; exceeding this displaces the earliest pinned link.
rules.pin-error-clear: When pin/unpin writes fail, the endpoint returns a clear error; failures are not disguised as successes.

tools.name: list_dashboard_links
tools.returns: { success, query, total, returned, dashboardLinks: [{ slug, title, url, description, icon, group, isPinned }], pinnedSlugs, maxPinnedLinks }
notes: Requires MCP user auth because each link summary includes the caller's current isPinned state.
Optional query reuses the web dashboard link search behavior across title, description, titlePinyin, and descriptionPinyin.
The MCP output exposes target URLs for client-side navigation but omits per-user click telemetry and internal pinyin search fields.

fields: Grid/list view mode persisted in browser storage

routes.returns: 200 JSON pin state when Accept requests JSON; otherwise 303 redirect
notes: JSON mode returns { pinnedSlugs: String[], maxPinnedLinks: Int, error: String? } for success and 400/401/500 failures.
Redirect mode returns to returnTo, adding dashboardLinkPinError=1 on failed writes.

routes.returns: { pinnedSlugs: String[], maxPinnedLinks: Int, error: String? }
notes: Applies up to 10 pin/unpin actions for the authenticated user in request order.
Uses the same pin limit and displacement behavior as the single pin endpoint.
Invalid or missing slug redirects to /.
Redirect is public; click count is recorded only when the request has an authenticated user.

tools.name: set_dashboard_link_pin_state
notes: Uses the shared dashboard link pin service and stores pins per authenticated MCP user.
Invalid slugs return { success: false, error: "invalid_slug", message, slug, pinnedSlugs, maxPinnedLinks } rather than an unexpected tool failure.
No MCP visit tool is exposed; visit redirects and click recording are browser/REST navigation behavior.
